topic: A person learns their college is closing down
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in my inbox with a sickening thud, the subject line a stark, unforgiving declaration: "Campus Closure Announcement." I stared at the screen, the words blurring into meaningless shapes. My hands started to shake so violently I had to grip the edge of my desk until my knuckles were white. Every sound in the dorm hallway—the distant slamming of a door, the hum of the fluorescent lights—seemed amplified, pressing in on me. I pushed my chair back, the scrape of metal on linoleum sounding deafening in the sudden silence of my room. My breath hitched, catching somewhere in my chest, and I backed away from the computer, stumbling until I hit the wall, sliding down until I was curled into a tight ball on the floor, trying to make myself small enough to disappear.

[story 2]

"This can't be real," I whispered, the sound thin and reedy in the echoing lecture hall. The official notice plastered on the bulletin board felt like a physical weight, pressing down on my shoulders. My stomach twisted into a cold, nauseating knot. I kept running my eyes over the names of the departments, each one a symbol of my entire future dissolving into dust. A bead of sweat traced a path down my temple, and I wiped it away frantically with the back of my hand, leaving a damp streak on my skin. I stood up abruptly, my legs feeling unsteady, and started pacing the length of the room, my footsteps quick and uneven, a frantic rhythm against the cavernous space.

[story 3]

I kept re-reading the fine print, my eyes skipping over the dense legal jargon as if hoping the truth would somehow vanish. A hollow sensation bloomed in my throat, making it hard to swallow. The reality of it—that four years, all the late-night study sessions, the friendships forged in shared exhaustion—was evaporating before my eyes. I dropped my backpack; it landed with a dull thud that seemed disproportionately loud. I looked around the empty common area, the familiar posters suddenly feeling like mocking ghosts. I pressed the heels of my palms against my eyes, squeezing them shut until a sharp, blinding pressure filled my vision, desperately trying to block out the sight of the closing doors.
